title: Minimal Example
text: vignette short explanation structure CI.RCT.Sim package. gives explanation contribute code data-generation scenarios well analysis functions. example uses simple example test estimator difference means two normal distributions. run simulations package SimDesign used. functions sumarisation simulation results re-used SimNPH package written previous simulation study.

title: Minimal Example
text: Let’s look code data generation. simulation study uses SimDesign pacakge. run simulations SimDesign, one define three functions Data Generation, Analysis Summarisation. functions use fixed parameters. function signatures can displayed SimDesign::SimFunctions(). first function generate_minimal_example displayed code . CI.RCT.Sim ’s two additional functions data-generating function. true_summary_statistics_minimal_example, calculates true (assymptotic) values summary statistics parameters given “Design” dataset. specific data generating model estimand. third function function prints outputs example Design dataset can used data generating functions. function gives dataset directly useable data generating function testing, documentation parameter users boilerplate code able cerate custom parameter ranges. R/generate_minimal_example.R true summary statistics really simple , just difference group means. utility function params_scenario_grid expands dataset following way: first row reference scenario first value defined parameter. subsequent rows contain scenarios parameter varied across given values parameters kept reference scenario value.
code: #' Generate dataset for minimal example #' #' @param condition row of Design dataset #' @param fixed_objects list of parameters that are fixed across simulations #' #' @returns a simulated dataset #' @export #' #' @examples #' Design <- assumptions_minimal_example() |> #' true_summary_statistics_minimal_example() #' #' dat <- generate_minimal_example(Design[1,]) #' head(dat) generate_minimal_example <- function(condition, fixed_objects=NULL){   data.frame(     group = c(rep(1, condition$n), rep(0, condition$n)),     y = c(rnorm(condition$n, condition$mean1), rnorm(condition$n, condition$mean0))   ) }  #' Calculate true summary statistics value for generate_minimal_example #' #' @param Design #' #' @returns For true_summary_statistics_minimal_example: the Design tibble with added column eff_size #' @export #' @describeIn generate_minimal_example calculate true effect size true_summary_statistics_minimal_example <- function(Design){   Design$eff_size <- Design$mean1 - Design$mean0    Design }  #' Create an empty assumtions tibble for generate_minimal_example #' #' @param print print code to generate parameter set? #' #' @returns For assumptions_mimimal_example: a design tibble with default values invisibly #' @export #' @describeIn generate_minimal_example generate default assumptions `tibble` assumptions_minimal_example <- function(print=interactive()){   skel <- "params_scenarios_grid(     n = c(50, 100), # n, pre group     mean1 = c(1,0), # mean, group 1     mean0 = 0       # mean, group 0   )"    if(print){     cat(skel)   }    invisible(     skel |>       str2expression() |>       eval()   ) }

title: Minimal Example
text: analysis functions used SimDesign take arguments condition dat, additional arguments can passed optional argument fixed_objects. running simulations current line Design dataset, containing parameters current simulation scenario passed condition argument. simulated dataset passed dat argument. analysis function called simulation replication scenario. able use hyper-parameters analysis methods, CI.RCT.Sim implements analysis functions functions hyper-parameters arguments return function can used SimDesign::RunSimulation. way one analysis method can easily used multiple times different hyper-parameters one simulation. minimal example two analysis functions defined, analyse_minimal_example_lm uses linear model analyse_minimal_example_t uses t-test. return value method named list enable summarisation later. R/analyse_minimal_example.R names names analysis results directly used analysis functions, others can choosen freely. “reserved” names : p p-value tests N_pat number recruited patients, relevant sequential adaptive designs N_evt number observed events, relevant time-event outcomes censoring
code: #' Analyse dataset from minimal example scenario with linear regression #' #' @param ci_level the confidence level for the CIs (defaults to 0.95) #' #' @returns an analyse function that returns a list with the elements #'  * `p` the p-value of the F-test #'  * `coef` the point estimate #'  * `ci_lower` the lower CI limit #'  * `ci_upper` the upper CI limit #' @export #' #' @examples #' Design <- assumptions_minimal_example() |> #'   true_summary_statistics_minimal_example() #' #' condition <- Design[1, ] #' #' dat <- generate_minimal_example(condition) #' #' my_analyse_lm <- analyse_minimal_example_lm(ci_level=0.9) #' my_analyse_lm(condition, dat) analyse_minimal_example_lm <- function(ci_level=0.95){   function(condition, dat, fixed_objects = NULL){     model <- lm(y~group, dat)     CI <- confint(model, level=ci_level)      list(       p = anova(model)$`Pr(>F)`[1],       coef = coefficients(model)["group"],       ci_lower = CI["group", 1],       ci_upper = CI["group", 2]     )   } }    #' Analyse dataset from minimal example scenario with t-test #' #' @returns an analyse function that returns a list with the elements #'  * `p` the p-value #' @export #' #' @examples #' Design <- assumptions_minimal_example() |> #'   true_summary_statistics_minimal_example() #' #' condition <- Design[1, ] #' #' dat <- generate_minimal_example(condition) #' #' my_analyse_t  <- analyse_minimal_example_t() #' my_analyse_t(condition, dat) analyse_minimal_example_t <- function(){   function(condition, dat, fixed_objects = NULL){     ttest <- t.test(dat$y[dat$group==1], dat$y[dat$group==0])      list(       p = ttest$p.value     )   } }

title: Minimal Example
text: actually run simulations, first dataset simulation parameters created true values summary statistics calculated necessary. list analysis functions created, hyper-parameters set, example coverage confidence intervals. list summary functions defined, analysis functions applied list outputs replications scenario. summary functions applied output analysis functions matched name function SimNPH::create_summary_function, names can repeated list summary functions. functions SimNPH::summarise_estimator SimNPH::summarise_test provide common summaries estimators tests, see documentation SimNPH package details. Finally SimDesign::runSimulation used conduct actual simulations. function takes care parallelisation, initialisation random seeds etc.
code: library("CI.RCT.Sim") #> Loading required package: SimDesign  # Define the parameter valued, calculate derived quantities from the parameters sim_parameters <- assumptions_minimal_example() |>   true_summary_statistics_minimal_example()  # constants for simulation and aggregation N_sim <- 1000 alpha <- 0.1  # list of analysis functions my_analyse <- list(   lm    = analyse_minimal_example_lm(ci_level=1-alpha),   ttest = analyse_minimal_example_t() )  # list of summarisation functions # those are applied to the results of the analysis functions by the same name, # repeating one name multiple times is allowed # summarise_estimator and summarise_test are generic summarisation function # from SimNPH, but own functions can be defined. my_summarise <- create_summarise_function(   lm    = summarise_estimator(est=coef, real=eff_size, lower=ci_lower, upper=ci_upper, null=0),   ttest = summarise_test(alpha),   lm    = function(condition, results, fixed_objects = NULL){     data.frame(mean_ci_width=mean(results$ci_upper - results$ci_lower))   } )  # run the simulations using SimDesign::runSimulation results <- runSimulation(   sim_parameters,   replications = N_sim,   generate = generate_minimal_example,   analyse = my_analyse,   summarise = my_summarise )  # inspect the results results |>   subset(select=c(names(sim_parameters), "lm.bias", "lm.sd_est", "ttest.rejection_0.1", "lm.1.mean_ci_width")) #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>       n mean1 mean0 eff_size    lm.bias lm.sd_est ttest.rejection_0.1 #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>               <dbl> #> 1    50     1     0        1 -0.0022700   0.19598               1     #> 2   100     1     0        1 -0.0038767   0.14157               1     #> 3    50     0     0        0  0.010151    0.20418               0.104 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: lm.1.mean_ci_width <dbl>
